---
title: Shiptons Heating and Cooling and Ransom and Blackmail Company
author: Jeffrey S. Racine
date: 2024-12-20
draft: false
feed: true
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

# Brutal Experience, Is Upselling The New Norm?

File this experience under the "fool me three times, shame on me" category.

We have a home with two furnaces and one a/c unit. We bought well built energy efficient units designed to last, and decided to take out a service contract that promised savings by bundling. The company (Shiptons Heating and Cooling) had a good reputation we were told (a decade ago that is), and they generally show up as scheduled, test the units, replace humidifier pads and clean and service the units annually. We pay $29/month for the contract.

## Strike One (Unnecessary Repairs)

In October 2023 during the routine maintenance of our furnaces, the tech (Peter Tambeau) told us the unit was showing its age, and in the written summary of his visit wrote "immediate repair, replace collector box". 

I contacted Shiptons dispatch and was told "I can confirm the price of the Collector Box is $417.60+H.S.T. (which includes the installation).  That price will be honoured as originally quoted by our Technician, Pete. As discussed, upon arrival of the part, I'll be directly in touch with Mrs. Racine & yourself to coordinate a date/time for the repair.  Please don't hesitate in contacting us if we can be of any further assistance in the interim."

Here is my email to Shiptons after Pete showed up at the house to do the repair:

> This morning the tech arrived to install the collector box we were advised to fix (the same tech who serviced the unit recently and wrote on the attached summary “immediate repair” Collector Box $417+HST). When he arrived he said “I don’t know why I am here, replacing the collector box won’t fix the leak in the unit”… I then said another tech had come and inspected the unit to get the part number, and he said “no one talked to me, I noted the part number.”  

> I asked him why he wrote “immediate repair collector box” and his reply was “they tell us to write something”. In other words, he is claiming that the collector box did not have a leak yet he wrote that this should be repaired immediately. 

> This is unacceptable. [...] Shiptons has dispatched two techs to deal with an issue clearly indicated as “Immediate repair” wasting money on two trips not to mention our time and frustration. The purpose of this email is to request that, going forward, kindly put a note on our file to not dispatch this technician to our residence (Peter Tambeau). 

> Thanks for your understanding.

So, chalk this visit up to "unnecessary repairs recommended"...

## Strike Two (Ransom)

In June 2024 a technician was dispatched for the annual maintenance of the a/c unit (Stephen Malgo). 

At the end of the service call, and with zero prior discussion, he said he had completed the maintenance, and had disabled the a/c from the exterior of the house, and would arrange for Stephen Shipton to come to our house that afternoon to discuss replacement options. The a/c unit had been operating perfectly (and, it still does for the record). I was stunned, and told him the unit works fine. He said it is low on refrigerant and there could be a leak in the condenser pan. I said Shiptons installed shut off devices to prevent leak damage, which he was aware of. I instructed him to go outside and enable the a/c unit. We had no call from Steven Shipton, but the next day were told someone else (Daniel Arcari) came to the house and we were not home (so he left). So he came back and said the unit was old, was low on refrigerant and could not be recharged so our only option was to replace. I asked for a quote out of curiosity and received weeks later a short quote in an email "Hey Jeffrey, I spoke with Scott and we figured out more or less what needs to be done. The cost to replace the 3 ton air conditioner and the air handler unit is. $15,395 plus HST". 

On July 3 2024 I replied to Daniel's quote writing "Would you kindly send the details for the quote you kindly provided? We were expecting more than a simple number, in particular the make/model of the a/c and air handler to be installed, cost of all other parts needed for the installation, and a breakdown of the $15,395 total into parts and labor, etc."

Crickets... zero response to date.

So, chalk this visit up to "we hijacked your a/c unit and are requesting ransom - the cost of replacement"...


## Strike Three (Blackmail)

In December 2024 we had our annual furnace maintenance. 

The technician arrived late (Josh Pattynama) came to service the units. On the invoice he present he wrote "immediate repair: 1) collector box ($470.25+HST), 2) venting repair ($1,399+HST), and 3) it may be time to have a home comfort adviser provide options on a more efficient system".

All good, right? 

Then he presents us with a "Safety Violation Notice" giving us 30 days to have his company to complete the repairs, or else? 

I kid you not (a scan of the front page of the PDF, and the document are below, you can scroll them to view them in their entirety):

::: {layout-row="2"}
![](images/notice.pdf){width="400"}

![](images/form.pdf){width="400"}
:::

In order to get Josh off of our property (we are undergoing a kitchen renovation), we had our contractor take time out of his day to go into the basement with him to review the issue, and only after that did Josh leave saying that we might "revisit this in the New Year".

I then sent the following to Shiptons:

> Greetings [],

> After the third service visit in a row (specifically, scheduled maintenance last Fall, this Spring, and now this Fall) that were some of the most unpleasant experiences my wife or I have had with any company, ever, we are left with no choice but to terminate our longstanding relationship with your company.
 
> Kindly cancel our service agreement, the automatic monthly withdrawals from our account, and also please send confirmation of so doing at year earliest convenience.
 
> Thanks for your understanding, I look forward to receiving confirmation and we appreciate your timely attention to this unpleasant matter.
 
So far, crickets...

# Epilogue

Signs were there all along, which perhaps we should have heeded? 5 years ago, a very nice tech was dispatched to do the maintenance. I asked how Shiptons was treating her and she said she was going to work for another company. She added without any prompting that we have really good equipment, and that Shiptons will try to upsell us and advises the techs to do the same which was one reason she was leaving. Another sign was from a former employee who told us the owners and management at Shiptons use high pressure tactics and reward techs who do the same. And after this event, we had a company refereed by our contractor come in to examine the offending equipment. And do you know what he noticed first after inspecting the system? He had taken pictures and said the furnaces needed a good cleaning, they were really filthy. We told him they were just cleaned and maintained yesterday, and he said we need to get them back to do the job properly. Enough said.
